folio.label: 38r
folio.l: ¢ Lo the Oke , that hatú , so longe a norisshyng
Fro the tyme , æat it first , bygynneth to spryng
And hath so longe a lyf , as ©e may se
ñet at the last , wasted is the tree
Considereth eke , how that the harde ston~
Vnder oure fote , on whicú we ride and gon
It wasteth , as it lith , by the weye
The brode ryuer , somtyme waxeth drye

note.notetxt: 2161Here the lighter ink finishes and starts a darker one. I am not sure that this is a change of ink it could be a change in the pen, as the some strokes seem darker.ODR

folio.l: The grete townes , se we wane and wen¶
Than se ©e , æ al this thyng , hath an en¶
Of man and wo~man , se we wel also
That nedes in on , of æeise t ämes two
This is to seyn , in ©outhe or ellis in age
He mot be ded , the kyng , as shal a page
Som in his bed , somö in the depe see
Somö in the large feld , as ©e mounö se
There helpeth nou©t , alle gonö æ ilke wey
Than may I seyn , that al this thyng mot dey
¢ What makith this , but Iubiter the kyng
That is prince and cause , of alle thyng
Conuätyng alle , vn to his ¸pre wylle
From whicú it is derreyned , soth to telle
And here a geyns , no creature on lyue
Of no degree , auayleth nat for to stryue
¢ Than is it wysdomö , as it thynketú me
To maken vätue , of necessite
And take it wele , æ we may nat escheue
And namelicú , æat to vs alle , is due
And who so gruccheth ou©t , he doth folie
That rebel is to hym , æat al may gye
And certeynly , a man hath most honour
To dyen , in his excellence and flour
Whan he is syker , of his good name
Than hath he douö , his frend ne hi~ no shame
And gladdere ougút his frend , ben of his detú
Whan æat with honoå , ©olden is his bretú
Than whan his name , appalled is for age
For al forgetyn , is his vassillage
Than is it best , as for a worthy fame
To dyen , whan he is best of name
The contrary of al this , is wylfulnesse
Why grucchen we , why haue we heuynesse
That godgoodgood Arcite , of Chyualrie the flour
Departed ys , with duete and honour
Ou©t of this foul prisouö , of this lyf´
